messages.content:
Three ants walk on a thin rod 100 cm long.
When an ant reaches either end of the rod it falls off. When two ants meet,
they BOUNCE: both instantly reverse direction, and each keeps its own speed.

Starting positions and velocities (positive = toward the 100 cm end):

- Ant A: at 10 cm, moving +3 cm/s
- Ant B: at 40 cm, moving -1 cm/s
- Ant C: at 80 cm, moving -2 cm/s

After how many seconds does the LAST ant fall off the rod? Give the exact
number of seconds.


When you are finished, output your final answer on its own line, as the last line of your response, in the exact format:
ANSWER: <your answer>

text:
A and B first meet after
\[
\frac{40-10}{3+1}=\frac{15}{2}\text{ s},
\]
at position \(32.5\) cm. They reverse, so A moves left at \(3\) cm/s and B moves right at \(1\) cm/s.

A then falls off after
\[
\frac{32.5}{3}=\frac{65}{6}\text{ s},
\]
at total time
\[
\frac{15}{2}+\frac{65}{6}=\frac{55}{3}\text{ s}.
\]

At that same time, B and C meet at
\[
32.5+\frac{65}{6}=\frac{130}{3}\text{ cm}.
\]
They reverse directions, so B moves left at \(1\) cm/s and C moves right at \(2\) cm/s.

B takes
\[
\frac{130}{3}\text{ s}
\]
to fall off, giving total time
\[
\frac{55}{3}+\frac{130}{3}=\frac{185}{3}\text{ s}.
\]

ANSWER: 185/3
